timeline.what: Born in Pretoria, South Africa
timeline.context: Born to Maye and Errol Musk; later became one of the most influential entrepreneurs of the 21st century

timeline.what: Severe bullying during school years; emotionally abusive father
timeline.context: Hospitalized after being thrown down stairs; described himself as 'the youngest and smallest kid in class' who was 'a prime target'; later called father Errol 'a terrible human being'

timeline.what: Self-taught programming; created video game Blastar at age 12
timeline.context: Got Commodore VIC-20 and taught himself to code; sold Blastar to South African magazine for ~$500; scored highest on IBM aptitude test

timeline.what: Emigrated to Canada at 17 to avoid South African military service
timeline.context: Left without parents' financial support; attended Queen's University in Ontario before transferring to University of Pennsylvania

timeline.what: Dropped out of Stanford PhD after 2 days to join Internet revolution
timeline.context: Enrolled in energy physics PhD but left immediately; cited desire to 'do something meaningful in the world' rather than complete PhD

timeline.what: Co-founded Zip2 with brother Kimbal; company sold for $307 million
timeline.context: Coded every night, often slept at office in Palo Alto; received $22 million from Compaq acquisition; later investigation revealed potential immigration status issues during this period

timeline.what: Founded X.com, online financial services company
timeline.context: Using Zip2 proceeds, Musk founded X.com

timeline.what: X.com merged with Confinity (PayPal), Musk ousted as CEO
timeline.context: Bititer power struggle; while Musk was on honeymoon, board replaced him with Peter Thiel

timeline.what: Founded SpaceX with $100 million of his own money
timeline.context: After 2001 Russia trip to buy ICBMs failed, Musk calculated he could build rockets for 10% of market rate

timeline.what: Musk's first child Nevada Alexander died at 10 weeks from sudden infant death syndrome.
timeline.context: Musk reportedly returned to work almost immediately after the tragedy.

timeline.what: Musk took over as CEO of Tesla during financial crisis
timeline.context: Became largest shareholder in 2004 with $6.3M investment; took CEO role when company was in severe financial distress

timeline.what: SpaceX fourth Falcon 1 launch succeeded - first privately developed liquid-fuel rocket to reach orbit
timeline.context: First three launches failed; after third failure, Musk had money for only one more attempt

timeline.what: NASA awarded SpaceX $1.6 billion contract for 12 cargo flights to ISS
timeline.context: Came when SpaceX and Tesla were nearly bankrupt; Musk had to borrow money to pay rent

timeline.what: In various interviews, Musk describes entrepreneurship in visceral terms and expresses willingness to take extreme personal financial risk
timeline.context: Pre-Tesla/SpaceX peak era, establishing his public philosophy on risk and persistence

timeline.what: 在TED访谈中解释第一性原理思维方法
timeline.context: Musk阐述了他如何将物理学框架应用于商业决策，从基本事实而非类比推理解决问题

timeline.what: Published 57-page Hyperloop Alpha white paper，proposing a high-speed transportation system capable of moving passengers at nearly 800 mph
timeline.context: Musk described it as 'a fifth mode of transportation' and chose to open-source the concept rather than building it himself, citing being too busy with SpaceX and Tesla

timeline.what: 在MIT研讨会上发出AI风险警告，称其为"召唤恶魔"
timeline.context: Musk表达对不受监管的AI开发的担忧，此后于2015年共同创立OpenAI

timeline.what: Musk co-founds Neuralink with $100M investment; also predicts fully autonomous cross-country Tesla drives by 2017
timeline.context: Diversification into neurotechnology and continued push on Tesla Autopilot capabilities

timeline.what: 在墨西哥国际宇航大会上发表火星殖民计划演讲
timeline.context: Musk提出将火星移民成本降至约20万美元（美国 median house price），并承认首批旅程风险极高

timeline.what: Tesla engineers resign over Musk's insistence on 'Autopilot' and 'Full Self-Driving' branding, warning of safety risks to customers
timeline.context: Internal conflict between Musk's marketing vision and engineering safety concerns

timeline.what: Musk founded The Boring Company to build underground tunnels
timeline.context: Venturing into infrastructure/transportation with a novel tunneling approach

timeline.what: Musk tweets 'Nobody ever changed the world on 40 hours a week' and describes 80-100 hour work weeks as necessary; sells 20,000 Boring Company flamethrowers at $500 each
timeline.context: Consolidation of his extreme work ethic brand and unconventional marketing tactics

timeline.what: SpaceX成功发射猎鹰重型火箭，将特斯拉Roadster送入太空
timeline.context: 测试载荷为Musk的红色特斯拉敞篷车，车内"Starman"假人播放David Bowie的《Space Oddity》

timeline.what: Model 3生产危机：Musk住在工厂车间，每周工作120小时，与工人同甘共苦
timeline.context: 特斯拉面临生产目标未达危机，Musk亲自驻厂督战

timeline.what: Musk发推文称考虑以420美元将特斯拉私有化，SEC起诉其证券欺诈
timeline.context: 推文引发市场波动，SEC指控该推文虚假且误导

timeline.what: 接受Joe Rogan播客访谈，阐述个人哲学
timeline.context: Musk表示"我们有责任维护意识的火光"，并称"我只想思考未来而不是悲伤"

timeline.what: Musk activated Starlink in Ukraine following a direct request from Ukrainian officials.
timeline.context: Provided critical internet access to Ukrainian military and civilians during Russian invasion.

timeline.what: Musk完成440亿美元Twitter收购，解雇约80%员工
timeline.context: Musk称"鸟已解放"，之后进行大规模裁员

timeline.what: Musk在DealBook峰会上怒斥广告商："去你的"
timeline.context: 广告商因内容审核问题撤离，Musk以粗俗言语回应

timeline.what: Musk became the largest individual political donor in U.S. history, donating $288 million to Republican candidates.
timeline.context: Publicly endorsed Trump after July assassination attempt; ran $1 million daily sweepstakes for registered voters.

timeline.what: Neuralink implants first brain chip in a human patient, who subsequently controls a computer cursor with his thoughts
timeline.context: Achievement of major milestone in brain-machine interface development

timeline.what: Musk designated as special government employee leading the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).
timeline.context: Applied corporate approach to government: mass layoffs, system shutdowns, rapid restructuring of federal agencies.

timeline.what: Musk stepped down from DOGE role.
timeline.context: A federal judge had ruled he acted as the 'de facto leader' of DOGE.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk chose to live with abusive father after parents divorced, later called this a mistake
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: 'Living with him was not a happy childhood' and later called living with his father 'a mistake'
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Consistently reported across multiple biographical accounts

evidence_cards.summary: Musk described his childhood as deeply unhappy with an emotionally abusive father
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: 'I was the youngest and smallest kid in class... a prime target' and called his father 'a terrible human being'
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Direct quotes from multiple biographies including Walter Isaacson 2023; consistent across sources

evidence_cards.summary: Created and sold a video game at age 12, demonstrating early technical aptitude
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: Created Blastar, sold to South African magazine for approximately $500; scored so high on IBM aptitude test it 'couldn't be properly assessed by the standard rubric'
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Reported in Ashlee Vance biography with specific details; relatively uncontroversial factual claim

evidence_cards.summary: Explicitly chose to leave South Africa to avoid participating in apartheid regime through military conscription
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: 'I didn't want to be conscripted into the army to fight for apartheid'
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Consistently cited reason for emigration across biographical sources

evidence_cards.summary: Musk dropped out of Stanford PhD citing stronger pull toward 'doing something meaningful in the world'
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: 'I could either watch the Internet revolution happen or I could be part of it... doing a PhD didn't feel like it was going to help the world in any significant way'
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Widely reported decision with consistent quotes across interviews; career-defining choice

evidence_cards.summary: Co-founded Zip2 and worked intensely, sleeping at office while coding nightly
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Reported by Ashlee Vance biography; corroborated by business records of Compaq acquisition

evidence_cards.summary: Washington Post investigation revealed potential unauthorized work during early entrepreneurial period
evidence_cards.reliability_note: 2024 Washington Post investigation; potential reputational risk but sourced investigation

evidence_cards.summary: Zip2 acquired by Compaq for $307 million; Musk received $22 million
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Documented in Washington Post investigation 2024 and Ashlee Vance biography; verifiable business transaction

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's comment on being ousted while on honeymoon
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: That's the problem with vacations.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Direct quote from Musk in biography; context clear

evidence_cards.summary: Musk calculated rocket building costs on flight home from Russia
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: On the flight home, Musk opened a spreadsheet and calculated the raw material costs of building a rocket from scratch. He concluded he could build one for about 10% of the going rate.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Reported in Ashlee Vance biography; detailed account

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's assessment of SpaceX success probability
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I thought the chance of success was less than 10%. I just accepted that I would probably lose everything.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Multiple interviews and biography confirm this quote

evidence_cards.summary: Musk became Tesla's largest shareholder with $6.3 million investment
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: Musk became Tesla's largest shareholder in 2004 with a $6.3 million investment. He became chairman of the board.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Confirmed by Tesla S-1 filing

evidence_cards.summary: Colleague's recollection of Musk's determination after third failure
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I'd never seen Elon look that defeated. He said we would keep going until we ran out of money.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Colleague recollection cited in biography

evidence_cards.summary: Musk described financial stress during SpaceX crisis period
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I had to borrow money from friends just to pay rent.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Direct quote from Musk about this period

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's statement on choosing to invest all remaining money in Tesla
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I could either let Tesla die or put all my remaining money into it. That wasn't really a choice.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Direct quote from Musk; context of crisis clear

evidence_cards.summary: Falcon 1 became first privately developed liquid-fuel rocket to reach orbit
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: The fourth launch, on September 28, 2008, succeeded — making Falcon 1 the first privately developed liquid-fuel rocket to reach orbit.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Historical fact documented in multiple sources

evidence_cards.summary: NASA awarded SpaceX $1.6 billion commercial cargo contract
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: Two days before Christmas 2008, NASA awarded SpaceX a $1.6 billion contract for 12 cargo transport flights to the International Space Station.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: NASA press release confirms contract details

evidence_cards.summary: Musk characterized entrepreneurship as existential suffering, using visceral imagery
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: Starting a company is like staring into the abyss and eating glass.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Direct interview quote; widely cited and consistent with his documented behavior

evidence_cards.summary: Musk expressed willingness to go to extremes rather than accept failure
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I'd rather commit seppuku than fail.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Direct interview statement; demonstrates his attitude toward failure as unacceptable

evidence_cards.summary: Musk阐述其第一性原理思维源于物理学框架
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I tend to approach things from a physics framework. Physics teaches you to reason from first principles rather than by analogy. So I said, okay, what are the fundamental truths of this problem, and reason up from there. This is what SpaceX is based on — not saying 'rockets have always been expensive, so they always will be,' but asking 'what are rockets actually made of? What's the raw material cost?'
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Musk本人直接阐述其方法论核心，为第一手陈述

evidence_cards.summary: Critics identified a recurring pattern of proposing revolutionary ideas but leaving execution to others
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: proposing revolutionary ideas and leaving execution to others, which some saw as visionary and others as irresponsible distraction
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Secondary interpretation of behavioral pattern across multiple instances

evidence_cards.summary: Musk explained why he open-sourced Hyperloop instead of building it himself
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I was too busy with SpaceX and Tesla
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Direct self-attribution of decision rationale

evidence_cards.summary: Musk later stated his motivation was simply to prove feasibility
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I just wanted to show it was possible
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Later interview quote clarifying intent

evidence_cards.summary: Musk published Hyperloop Alpha white paper proposing a fifth mode of transportation
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: a high-speed transportation system capable of moving passengers at nearly 800 mph
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Primary source document by Musk himself

evidence_cards.summary: Musk明确警告AI风险，将其比作"召唤恶魔"
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: With artificial intelligence we are summoning the demon. You know all those stories where there's the guy with the pentagram and the holy water, and he's like, yeah, he's sure he can control the demon? Doesn't work out.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Musk在MIT研讨会上的直接表态，此观点此后持续影响其行动

evidence_cards.summary: Musk made a bold prediction about fully autonomous driving that did not materialize on the stated timeline
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: [In 2016 he predicted fully autonomous cross-country drives by 2017]
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Cross-referenced with public record; Musk made numerous autonomous driving timeline predictions that did not pan out

evidence_cards.summary: Musk在IAC大会上提出火星殖民的经济目标
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: The cost of moving to Mars needs to be roughly equivalent to the median house price in the US — around $200,000.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: 公开演讲原文，Musk对其商业目标的具体表述

evidence_cards.summary: Musk承认火星旅程的高度危险性
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: The first journeys to Mars will be very dangerous. The risk of fatality is quite high. There's no way around it. Are you prepared to die? If that's okay, then you're a candidate for going.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: 公开演讲原文，直接回应关于死亡风险的问题

evidence_cards.summary: Musk阐述多行星物种的二元选择
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: History is going to bifurcate along two directions. One path is we stay on Earth forever, and then there will be some eventual extinction event. The alternative is to become a space-faring civilization and a multi-planetary species.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: 公开演讲中的核心愿景表述

evidence_cards.summary: Musk framed Neuralink as a response to AI existential risk, merging human cognition with machines
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: If you can't beat them, join them. The merge with AI is the best-case scenario.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Neuralink presentations; aligns with Musk's stated public rationale for the venture

evidence_cards.summary: Musk credits Douglas Adams with teaching him that formulating the right question is harder than finding answers
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: 'Once you can properly phrase the question, the answer is the easy part' — describing Hitchhiker's Guide as formative influence during existential crisis at age 12-15
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Direct interview quote from TED 2017; a key self-reported influence

evidence_cards.summary: Musk insisted on 'Autopilot' and 'Full Self-Driving' branding despite engineering warnings that it endangered lives
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Wall Street Journal reporting; engineers' warnings documented in Tesla communications

evidence_cards.summary: Musk articulated extreme work ethic as prerequisite for changing the world
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: Nobody ever changed the world on 40 hours a week.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Direct self-attributed statement by Musk via his primary public channel

evidence_cards.summary: Musk fired engineers on the spot for disagreeing with him, sometimes rehiring them days later
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Based on multiple former employee accounts in Tim Higgins' 'Power Play'; consistent with known management style

evidence_cards.summary: Musk used unconventional marketing (selling flamethrowers) to fund ventures before tangible products existed
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: It was obviously a terrible idea [but] they sold out immediately.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Self-admitted by Musk; also sold 50,000 hats before the company had dug a single tunnel

evidence_cards.summary: Musk对太空特斯拉的浪漫想象
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I love the thought of a car drifting apparently endlessly through space and perhaps being discovered by an alien race millions of years in the future.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: 多家媒体引用的Musk原话

evidence_cards.summary: Musk移除昂贵的自动化输送系统，改用人工劳动
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: Excessive automation at Tesla was a mistake. Humans are underrated.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: 本人对决策的解释，直接引用

evidence_cards.summary: Musk经常绕过工程团队做生产决策
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: He personally intervened in production line design, often overriding his engineering team's recommendations.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: 传记作家Isaacson报道，有多个消息源

evidence_cards.summary: Musk表示愿与工人同甘共苦
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: If the workers are suffering, I want to suffer with them. My suffering should be greater than theirs.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: 本人亲口陈述，直接引用

evidence_cards.summary: Musk发推称资金已到位考虑私有化
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: Am considering taking Tesla private at $420. Funding secured.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: 本人推文原文，SEC文件引用

evidence_cards.summary: SEC起诉Musk证券欺诈，最终达成和解
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: The SEC sued Musk for securities fraud, alleging the tweet was false and misleading.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: SEC起诉状原文，WSJ报道

evidence_cards.summary: Musk否认自己是救世主
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I'm not trying to be anyone's savior. I'm just trying to think about the future and not be sad.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Joe Rogan播客中的自我定位澄清

evidence_cards.summary: Musk阐述对人类意识的责任
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I think we have a duty to maintain the light of consciousness, to make sure it continues into the future.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Joe Rogan播客采访中的直接表述

evidence_cards.summary: Musk restricted Starlink near Crimea to avoid complicity in war
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: If I had agreed to their request, SpaceX would be explicitly complicit in a major act of war.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Attributed via Isaacson biography

evidence_cards.summary: Musk secretly restricted Starlink's range near Crimea, preventing Ukrainian attack on Russian naval vessels
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Multiple news reports, Isaacson biography

evidence_cards.summary: Musk解雇Twitter约80%员工并要求"极度硬核"工作
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: He sent an email giving remaining employees an ultimatum: commit to extremely hardcore work or leave with severance.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: 内部邮件泄露，新闻报道确认

evidence_cards.summary: Musk完成收购后发推"鸟已解放"
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: the bird is freed
evidence_cards.reliability_note: 本人推文原文

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's pragmatic response to personal tragedy
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I'm not sure what sitting around being sad is going to do.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Reported via colleague; Isaacson biography attribution

evidence_cards.summary: When asked if he was happy, Musk acknowledged emotional solitude
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: There are many nights I sleep alone.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Direct interview quote, but vague context

evidence_cards.summary: Isaacson documents Musk's recurring 'demon mode' management style
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: When Elon is in demon mode, he will fire people, destroy entire teams' work, demand impossible deadlines — and then somehow the product ships.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Isaacson biographical account, draws from multiple sources

evidence_cards.summary: Musk对广告商爆粗口："去你的"
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: If someone's going to try to blackmail me with advertising, blackmail me with money? Go fuck yourself. Is that clear? I hope it is.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: DealBook峰会现场视频记录

evidence_cards.summary: WSJ评价Twitter收购为2008年来最差并购
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: the worst deal in merger finance since the 2008 financial crisis
evidence_cards.reliability_note: WSJ财经分析，引用市场估值数据

evidence_cards.summary: Musk became the largest individual political donor in U.S. history
evidence_cards.reliability_note: FEC filings provide verifiable donation records

evidence_cards.summary: Musk frames his political shift as the Democrats abandoning him
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I used to be a moderate Democrat. I didn't leave the Democrats. The Democrats left me.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Direct interview quote

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's confident outlook on DOGE's lasting impact
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: The DOGE mission will only strengthen over time as it becomes a way of life throughout the government.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Public statement at resignation, multiple news corroboration
